trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed. Sweden has implemented EU sanctions fully and expelled or reduced Russian diplomatic presence. Swedish companies (IKEA, Volvo, Electrolux, Sandvik, Ericsson) have fully exited Russian operations — representing billions in asset write-downs. Pre-2022 Sweden-Russia trade was substantial given Sweden's position as an advanced industrial exporter and Russia's appetite for European machinery and technology.
disputes: Russia threatened Sweden following NATO accession announcement
Disputes over Nord Stream sabotage investigation (Sweden investigated, Russia accused of cover-up)

diplomatic.status: hostile — Sweden joined NATO in 2024, Russia has made explicit threats
diplomatic.summary: Russia-Sweden relations have reached a historic low following Sweden's accession to NATO in March 2024 — ending 200+ years of Swedish neutrality/non-alignment. Russia explicitly threatened consequences for Sweden's NATO membership, including military countermeasures in the Baltic and Arctic regions. Sweden has expelled Russian intelligence officers, implemented full EU sanctions, and is one of Ukraine's strongest supporters per capita. The Nord Stream sabotage investigation (where Sweden was a key investigator) added another layer of mutual hostility. Russia has designated Sweden an adversary.
recent_incidents: Russia made explicit military threats following Swedish NATO bid (2022)
Sweden joined NATO March 2024 — Russia announced countermeasures
Nord Stream investigation: Sweden closed its investigation (2024) without fully attributing blame, angering Russia which demanded access
Russian military aircraft provocations in Swedish air defense identification zone

military.summary: Sweden is now a NATO ally, which Russia designates as a direct threat to its northwestern flank. Russia has increased Baltic Sea military activity, conducted provocative exercises, and simulated strikes on Swedish territory in military planning. Sweden has dramatically increased defense spending, reactivated Gotland Island military garrison (of significant Baltic strategic importance), and is integrating deeply into NATO command structures. Russian submarines operate in Swedish maritime zones. All bilateral military contacts have been severed.
military.alliance_status: adversarial — Sweden is NATO, Russia is designated adversary
incidents: Russia simulated military strikes on Swedish military targets in exercises
Russian submarines detected in Swedish territorial waters
Russian aircraft provocations in Swedish ADIZ
Russia threatened to deploy additional Baltic Fleet assets in response to Swedish NATO membership

financial.summary: All significant financial ties severed. Swedish companies have written off Russian assets. Swedish banks (Swedbank had significant Baltic-Russia exposure) have exited Russian markets. Russian assets in Sweden are frozen. No financial flows of significance remain.

energy.summary: Sweden has fully eliminated Russian energy dependency. Sweden was historically not heavily reliant on Russian fossil fuels (high nuclear and hydro share), making the transition relatively smooth. Nord Stream pipelines that ran near Swedish waters are destroyed. No bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: All formal scientific cooperation has been suspended. Swedish universities have terminated partnerships with Russian institutions. Sweden participates in academic sanctions aligned with EU guidance on Russian institutions. Some historical collaboration in Arctic research is now completely discontinued.
